wordlist.desc: To admonish someone is to tell them you disapprove of their behavior.
wordlist.exam: The teacher admonished Mark because he was chewing gum in class.

wordlist.desc: If something is audible, then it is able to be heard.
wordlist.exam: The sound of the drums was audible from miles away.

wordlist.desc: If something or someone is awesome, they are impressive or frightening.
wordlist.exam: The huge military plane was an awesome sight.

wordlist.desc: To beware means to be careful of something or someone that is dangerous.
wordlist.exam: You should beware of driving fast on wet roads.

wordlist.desc: To brag means to talk of one’s abilities or achievements in a proud way.
wordlist.exam: He had strong muscles and bragged about it to the entire class.

wordlist.desc: If someone is conscious of something, then they are aware of it.
wordlist.exam: The new student was conscious of the other students staring at her.

wordlist.desc: To disagree with someone means to have a different opinion from them.
wordlist.exam: The lawyers disagreed about the best way to settle the case.

wordlist.desc: To echo means that a sound repeats itself because it bounced off an object.
wordlist.exam: The child yelled over the canyon, and the wall echoed the sound.

wordlist.desc: If something is eventual, it will happen at the end of a series of events.
wordlist.exam: The constant training and planning led the team to an eventual victory.

wordlist.desc: A hint is information that suggests something will happen or is true.
wordlist.exam: I quietly passed on a hint to my sister about the test.

wordlist.desc: An idiot is a person who is not smart or who has done something silly.
wordlist.exam: Because he got lost in the forest, the man felt like an idiot.

wordlist.desc: If something is immense, it is very large.
wordlist.exam: An immense amount of money was needed to buy such a large boat.

wordlist.desc: If something is indirect, then it is not the easiest or straightest way.
wordlist.exam: He chose to take the most indirect route to the coast.

wordlist.desc: An option is a choice between two or more things.
wordlist.exam: The children were given the option of three houses to pick from.

wordlist.desc: A pastime is an activity done for fun that you do often.
wordlist.exam: In the US, baseball is considered the national pastime.

wordlist.desc: If something is perfect, then it is without any mistakes.
wordlist.exam: She got all the questions right, so her score was perfect.

wordlist.desc: To pinpoint something means to locate it exactly.
wordlist.exam: The navigation system in my car is able to pinpoint my exact location.

wordlist.desc: To switch means to change something to something else.
wordlist.exam: Mom switched the TV station from the news to her favorite show.

wordlist.desc: If something or someone is thorough, then they are complete in every way.
wordlist.exam: Tina did a thorough job of cleaning the stains out of the carpet.

wordlist.desc: To torment someone means to cause them to suffer on purpose.
wordlist.exam: She tormented her little brother by taking his favorite toy.

exercise.story:
Choose the word that is the better fit for each blank.

  1. idiot / option
    I had the __________ of going with them or staying home. I felt like an __________ for the choice I made.
  2. admonished / torment
    Because he liked to __________ the kitten by pouring water on it, the boy’s mother __________ him for his cruel behavior.
  3. echoed / pinpoint
    It was difficult to __________ the dog’s location because its cries __________ off of the hills.
  4. brag / perfect
    My sister likes to __________ about how my mother and father think her behavior is __________.
  5. beware / switch
    When you __________ the machine on, you need to __________ because the machine is dangerous.
  6. eventual / pastime
    All of the accidents at the swimming pool led to the __________ closing of it and the end of a favorite summer __________.
  7. disagreed / awesome
    The king wanted people to think that his power was __________. He would put people in jail if they __________ with him.
  8. audible / conscious
    Even though the little girl was trying to sneak up on her parents, her __________ steps made her parents __________ of her.
  9. immense / indirect
    An __________ tree fell across the road, forcing the travelers to take a more __________ route through the countryside.
  10. hint / thorough
    The police could not find a single clue to the crime, which gave a __________ as to how __________ the thief had been.

Part A: Match the phrases to make complete sentences.

  1. a. about which game was better
  2. b. of the hole in the bridge
  3. c. for talking during the test
  4. d. was the reason for the bad smell
  5. e. about how good she is at sports
  6. f. of the sick man’s health
  7. g. took an hour more to drive
  8. h. fear that his home would be damaged
  9. i. from her left hand to her right
  10. j. is listening to music
  1. You should beware __________.
  2. My favorite pastime __________.
  3. The doctor did a thorough check __________.
  4. The two friends disagreed __________.
  5. She switched the bag __________.
  6. His proud sister brags __________.
  7. An immense pile of garbage __________.
  8. The indirect route __________.
  9. The awesome storm caused __________.
  10. She admonished the student __________.

Part B: Match the clauses to make complete sentences.

  1. a. and it led to her eventual illness
  2. b. because they gave him so many hints
  3. c. if they wanted to get a perfect grade
  4. d. but he was conscious of the other boats
  5. e. because bad dreams tormented him
  6. f. then people might think you’re an idiot
  7. g. but it was just the echo of her voice
  8. h. because there weren’t any other options
  9. i. so he could pinpoint his location in the wild
  10. j. because the sound of the bone breaking was audible
  1. He couldn’t sleep at night __________.
  2. We knew he broke his leg when he fell __________.
  3. He had to choose the blue car __________.
  4. The hiker always carried a compass and map, __________.
  5. She didn’t eat good food and didn’t exercise, __________.
  6. The students had to answer all the questions correctly __________.
  7. The captain did not see the ship approaching behind him, __________.
  8. She thought someone was calling to her from the other cliff, __________.
  9. He knew his friends were having a surprise party for him __________.
  10. If you can’t tie your own shoes by the time you’re twelve years old __________.

Exercise 1
  1. option / idiot
  2. torment / admonished
  3. pinpoint / echoed
  4. brag / perfect
  5. switch / beware
  6. eventual / pastime
  7. awesome / disagreed
  8. audible /conscious
  9. immense / indirect
  10. hint / thorough
Exercise 2
Part A
  1. b
  2. j
  3. f
  4. a
  5. i
  6. e
  7. d
  8. g
  9. h
  10. c
Part B
  1. e
  2. j
  3. h
  4. i
  5. a
  6. c
  7. d
  8. g
  9. b
  10. f

reading.en: The Young Man and the Old Man
reading.story:
A proud young man was looking for a new pastime. He heard about people hiking in the national parks and decided to try it for himself. As he started his stroll, an old man walked up to him.

“Don’t go this way,” the old man said. “Beware. The paths are not clear. It’s easy to become lost.”

But the young man disagreed with the old man and bragged that he had a perfect understanding of the park. “I studied maps of this area,” he told him. “I believe I have a thorough knowledge of these trails. I won’t become lost.”

The old man listened to the young man and then admonished him for his pride.

“I have walked these trails my entire life,” he said. “If you think you will be safe, then go ahead.”

The young man ignored the old man and started along the trail.

Whenever he had to choose between an easy or difficult route, he always chose the more difficult option. In addition, he was not conscious of which direction he was going. After a while, he decided to return home. Because his course through the wilderness was so indirect, he had no idea where he was.

He looked at his map but could not pinpoint his location. He walked one path after another but soon realized he was lost.

The sun was going down, and sudden strong winds gave a hint that it might rain. Immense clouds filled the sky. Awesome sounds of thunder were audible from all directions. It echoed off the mountains. The thought of the eventual storm tormented the young man. He hurried in one direction, but soon switched out of confusion. Luckily, it led him out of the park.

When he arrived home, he knew that he had acted like an idiot. He realized he was lucky to be alive. He decided to listen to people with more experience than himself.

Part A: Mark each statement T for true or F for false. Rewrite the false statements to make them true.

  1. _____ The young man decided to hike in the national parks.
    __________
  2. _____ The young man disagreed with the old man.
    __________
  3. _____ The young man believed that his knowledge of the park’s trails was thorough.
    __________
  4. _____ When the young man had an option of two trails, he always chose the easier one.
    __________
  5. _____ The young man was tormented by the thought of the eventual storm.
    __________
  6. _____ When the young man got home, he knew that the old man had acted like an idiot.
    __________

Part B: Answer the questions.

  1. What did the young man choose as his new pastime?
    • a. Picking trails
    • b. Climbing mountains
    • c. Talking with old men
    • d. Hiking in parks
  2. Why did the old man admonish the young man?
    • a. The young man was awesome.
    • b. The young man bragged that he was perfect.
    • c. The young man’s route was too indirect.
    • d. The young man chose difficult trails.
  3. Why did the young man become lost?
    • a. He was not conscious of where he went.
    • b. He pinpointed his location on his map.
    • c. He did not switch to an easier trail.
    • d. He listened to the old man’s advice.
  4. Which of the following gave a hint of an eventual storm?
    • a. The sudden strong winds
    • b. The sparse clouds in the sky
    • c. The weather getting colder
    • d. The lightining flashing in the sky

Reading Comprehension
Part A
  1. T
  2. T
  3. T
  4. F / When the young man had an option of two trails, he always chose the more difficult of the two.
  5. T
  6. F / When the young man arrived home, he knew that he had acted like an idiot.
Part B
  1. d
  2. b
  3. a
  4. a
